core_mechanism: an asymptotic spectral-counting (Weyl-type) law for the resonances of a linear operator: the number of poles of its analytically continued resolvent inside a sector of the complex spectral plane, out to spectral radius lambda, grows as a power C*lambda^d, established by controlling the resolvent through a small (semiclassical) parameter and an analytic deformation (complex scaling) that turns resonances into isolated poles
brief_reason: The abstract proves a Weyl law showing the count of quasinormal modes (poles of the continued resolvent) in a sector below the real axis with modulus below lambda grows like C*lambda^3, obtained via uniform semiclassical resolvent estimates near threshold energy combined with complex scaling.
